Home / Forms & Surveys / Alternatives to Google Forms

2 open source alternatives to Google Forms

Free forms attached to Google Sheets. Here are the open source projects real teams use instead — ranked by fit, with honest pros and cons for each.

What people don't love about Google Forms

  • Basic styling options.
  • Data lives in Google cloud.
  • Limited logic branching.

Current Google Forms pricing (for reference): Free.

Ad slot — above comparison

Quick comparison

Alternative Best for License Self-host Hosted cloud?
Formbricks
Open source Typeform alternative with in-app surveys. 		Modern UI and in-app surveys where Google Forms feels old. AGPL-3.0 ★★☆☆☆ Yes
LimeSurvey
Long-established open source survey platform. 		Any survey where Google Forms' logic runs out of gas. GPL-2.0 ★★★☆☆ Yes

1. Formbricks — Modern UI and in-app surveys where Google Forms feels old.

Open source Typeform alternative with in-app surveys.

Strengths

  • Modern React UI.
  • In-app surveys for product teams.
  • Quick docker deploy.

Weaknesses

  • Some analytics tied to paid tier.
  • Younger project with evolving features.
  • Form logic UX is still improving.
License: AGPL-3.0 Self-host difficulty: 2/5 Hosted cloud option

Formbricks homepage · Source on GitHub · Google Forms vs Formbricks →

2. LimeSurvey — Any survey where Google Forms' logic runs out of gas.

Long-established open source survey platform.

Strengths

  • Deep question types and logic.
  • Used widely in research and academia.
  • Self-hosted or paid cloud.

Weaknesses

  • UI feels dated.
  • Setup wizard is clunky.
  • Theming requires effort to match modern brands.
License: GPL-2.0 Self-host difficulty: 3/5 Hosted cloud option

LimeSurvey homepage · Source on GitHub · Google Forms vs LimeSurvey →
